title: McKesson Corporation (MCK) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of McKesson Corporation (MCK) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 84/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: The 6.0% growth rate assumption reflects our balanced view on the company's ability to grow free cash flow over the next 5 years.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.0% discount rate is applied to reflect the risk profile and cost of capital.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: The 2.0% terminal growth rate is consistent with long-term macroeconomic expectations.

thesisParagraphs: McKesson Corporation is an essential pillar of the North American healthcare infrastructure. By delivering a third of all pharmaceutical products used in the region, it operates as a massive, highly efficient toll road that generates over $9.1B in predictable free cash flow. The company's strategic pivot toward higher-margin specialty distribution and health information technology is successfully offsetting the structural low margins (1.5% operating margin) of its core wholesale business.

Our valuation highlights McKesson's tremendous cash-generating ability. The combination of steady 11.4% top-line growth, disciplined cost management, and a relentless focus on share repurchases creates a compelling compounding machine. The stock appears attractively valued, offering a margin of safety for long-term investors within the defensive healthcare sector.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is the terminal growth rate set at 2.0%?
faq.answer: A 2.0% terminal growth rate reflects a mature, low-margin business that will grow roughly in line with long-term inflation and healthcare utilization trends.

faq.question: How does the discount rate of 8.0% reflect McKesson's risk profile?
faq.answer: The 8.0% discount rate accounts for the company's defensive characteristics and low beta of 0.35, while acknowledging regulatory and drug pricing risks in the healthcare sector.

faq.question: What is driving the 6% FCF growth assumption?
faq.answer: The 6% growth assumption is driven by the continued expansion of specialty pharmaceuticals, operating leverage, and the positive impact of share buybacks on per-share metrics.

prospectVerdictDetail: McKesson Corporation is the ninth-largest company by revenue in the US and a critical backbone of the healthcare system. It delivers a third of all pharmaceutical products used in North America. Despite razor-thin operating margins of 1.5%, its immense scale generates massive free cash flow exceeding $9.1B. With strong revenue growth of 11.4%, McKesson is well-positioned for sustained value creation.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: McKesson is experiencing strong momentum, driven by solid demand for pharmaceuticals, particularly in the specialty segment, and effective execution across its distribution network.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs Peers
factors.rationale: McKesson consistently delivers robust top-line growth, currently running at 11.4%. The expanding volume of prescription utilization acts as a continuous tailwind, allowing it to capture share in high-growth areas like specialty pharmaceuticals.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: As the nation's largest healthcare company, delivering a third of all pharmaceutical products in North America, McKesson maintains a deeply entrenched market share with significant barriers to entry.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Operating in a low-margin (1.5% operating margin) industry limits traditional pricing power. However, McKesson's immense scale allows it to effectively manage purchasing costs from manufacturers and protect its profitability.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The company continues to expand its services beyond traditional distribution, adding value-added health information technology and health management tools to drive customer stickiness.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: McKesson's economic moat is exceptionally wide, rooted in the massive scale of its distribution network and the critical nature of its services to the healthcare ecosystem.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Pharmacies and hospitals are highly integrated with McKesson's inventory and ordering systems. Switching to a competitor is complex, costly, and operationally risky, leading to very high retention rates.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Employing over 80,000 people and managing a vast network of distribution centers creates significant logistical advantages and density, lowering per-unit costs and creating a formidable barrier to entry.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The distribution industry is highly regulated. McKesson's established regulatory expertise and compliance infrastructure provide a competitive advantage over potential new entrants, despite historical legal risks.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The business generates massive free cash flow of $9.15B relative to its capital requirements. This capital-light model allows for consistent reinvestment and shareholder returns, supporting its $110B+ market cap.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Investor sentiment is largely positive, supported by the company's consistent earnings beats, share buyback programs, and the defensive nature of the healthcare sector.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts have generally revised earnings estimates upward, reflecting strong operational performance and the accretive impact of ongoing share repurchases, with a forward P/E of roughly 20x.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative remains constructive, focusing on the company's resilience as the nation's largest healthcare company and its strategic focus on higher-margin oncology and specialty distribution.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has demonstrated strong capital discipline, prioritizing high-return investments and aggressively returning capital to shareholders through buybacks, though the dividend yield is a modest 0.36%.

prospectKeyRisks: Changes in drug pricing legislation or reimbursement models could impact profitability and margins in the distribution segment.
The loss of a major pharmacy or hospital system customer could significantly impact revenue and scale advantages.
Ongoing legal and regulatory risks associated with the distribution of controlled substances, despite recent settlements.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued rapid growth in the specialty pharmaceutical and oncology segments, driving higher margin contributions.
Further expansion of the company's technology and service offerings, deepening integration with healthcare providers.
Sustained, aggressive share repurchase programs that continuously enhance earnings per share.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored MCK at 82/100 and Opus at 87/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
